title: podman build improperly creating intermediate images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman build is keeping intermediate layers around as it appears to default to

export BUILDAH_LAYERS=true

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Dockerfile with multiple RUN commands. As an example, the start of a centos:6 environment build I have

FROM		centos:6
MAINTAINER	Sean McNeil <sean@mcneil.com>
WORKDIR		/tmp

RUN	yum install -y wget
RUN	wget http://dl.fedoraproject.org/pub/epel/6/x86_64/epel-release-6-8.noarch.rpm
RUN	rpm -ivh epel-release-6-8.noarch.rpm
RUN	yum clean expire-cache
RUN	yum update -y

2. Create an image with "podman build --squash -t sean/centos:6 ."

3. Run "podman images" and see many layers.

4. No build command arguments affect the creation of the layers. I've tried --rm=true and --force-rm=true.

**Describe the results you received:**

Looks like 1 image is listed for each RUN command.

**Describe the results you expected:**

There should only be 2 layers that are created from the build. There should be the original layer copied with the FROM declaration and then the committed layer for the end of the build which is named "sean/centos:6".

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

If I add

export BUILDAH_LAYERS=false

I get the expected behavior.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.11.4
Git Commit:    "49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d"
Built:         Tue Jan 15 03:38:17 2019
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.6-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.0.0-1.git82e8011.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 49780a1cf10d572edc4e1ea3b8a8429ce391d47d'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "29"
  MemFree: 6140149760
  MemTotal: 33742360576
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-66.dev.gitbbb17ef.fc29.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc6+dev
      commit: ead425507b6ba28278ef71ad06582df97f2d5b5f
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 16636178432
  SwapTotal: 16852709376
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 16
  hostname: desk.mcneil.com
  kernel: 4.19.15-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 69h 9m 20.77s (Approximately 2.88 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /home/sean/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/sean/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 29 host

comments.body:
Hi @seanmcneil3, thanks a lot for opening the issue!

> Run "podman images" and see many layers.

The intermediate layers are only displayed via the **--all** option of `podman images` to not disturb the output.

> No build command arguments affect the creation of the layers. I've tried --rm=true and --force-rm=true

Those options have no effect on the layers as they relate to the intermediate containers (used to create intermediate layers and execute the Dockerfile) but not to the layers.

The reason why intermediate layers are kept is _caching_. If, for instance, RUN statement 4 changes, all previous steps don't need to be executed again as the intermediate layers are cached. But as stated in this issue, podman-build offers to opt-out via the `BUILDAH_LAYERS` environment variable.

Given there is a solution to the described issue (documented in the help message and the manpage) I am closing the bug.
